techs.name: The Beginning
techs.quote: 'Where tillage begins, other arts follow. The farmers therefore are the founders of human civilization.' - Daniel Webster

techs.name: Astronomy
uniques: Enables [Embarked] units to enter ocean tiles
techs.quote: We are no longer bound to our old world. It's now time to go forth to virgin land!

techs.name: Banking
techs.quote: 'I saw a bank that said ’24-Hour Banking,’ but I didn’t have that much time.' - Steven Wright

techs.name: Centralised Power
techs.quote: 'To better manage the evolution of complex empires—including wily lords with grand ambitions—a centralised system to collect and disburse taxes is necessary.' - Humankind

techs.name: Gunpowder
techs.quote: 'To better manage the evolution of complex empires—including wily lords with grand ambitions—a centralised system to collect and disburse taxes is necessary.' - Humankind

techs.name: ANCIENT
techs.quote: 'He who commands the sea has command of everything.' - Themistocles

techs.name: Movable Typeface
techs.quote: 'Advancements in metallurgy and metalworking allow the creation of rapidly replaceable typefaces, revolutionizing printing.' - Humankind

techs.name: Humanism
techs.quote: 'As wealthy empires grow, merchants and nobles express their power by funding artistic endeavors and scientific research.' - Humankind

techs.name: Chartered Companies
techs.quote: 'The creation of charters and shares allows companies to manage and take greater risks, in the hopes of greater profits.' - Humankind

techs.name: Colonialism
uniques: [+50]% Production when constructing [Settler] units [in all cities]
techs.quote: 'Colonialism. The enforced spread of the rule of reason. But who is going to spread it among the colonisers?' - Anthony Burgess

techs.name: Navigation
techs.quote: 'It’s not the towering sails, but the unseen wind that moves a ship.' - English Proverb

techs.name: Enlightenment
techs.quote: 'New opinions are always suspected, and usually opposed, without any other reason but because they are not already common.' - John Locke

techs.name: Mercantilism
techs.quote: 'A zero-sum trade system, this maximizes a nation's exports at the expense of other nations—and one's colonies.' - Humankind

techs.name: Mass Production
techs.quote: 'What can be labeled, packaged, mass produced is neither truth nor art.' - Marty Rubin

techs.name: Siege Cannons
techs.quote: 'Packed with frightening explosive power, in a few hours these new weapons can crumble city walls that have stood for millenia.' - Humankind

techs.name: Encyclopedia
techs.quote: 'The tome encompassing the sum of human knowledge is sought by the tyrant and the benevolent alike.' - Humankind

techs.name: Steam Power
techs.quote: 'It is arguable whether the human race have been gainers by the march of science beyond the steam engine.' - Winston Churchill

techs.name: Military Science
techs.quote: 'Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake.' - Napoleon Bonaparte

techs.name: Rifling
techs.quote: 'Never criticize a rifleman until you have walked a mile in his shoes. That way, he’ll be barefoot and you’ll be out of range.' - The 2nd Target Company

techs.name: Sanitation
techs.quote: 'No innovation in the past 200 years has done more to save lives and improve health than the sanitation revolution triggered by the invention of the toilet.' - Sylvia Burwell

techs.name: Archaeology
techs.quote: 'Had I been present at the Creation, I would have given some useful hints for the better ordering of the universe.' - Nelson Algren

techs.name: Economics
techs.quote: 'You and I come by road or rail, but economists travel on infrastructure.' - Margaret Thatcher

techs.name: Industrialization
techs.quote: 'Stronger, faster, more productive! Mechanization is the dream of tomorrow, today!' - Humankind

techs.name: Scientific Method
techs.quote: 'This distills thousands of years of speculation on how to discover natural truths into an instrument of razor-sharp precision.' - Humankind

techs.name: Ideology
techs.quote: 'You and I come by road or rail, but economists travel on infrastructure.' - Margaret Thatcher

techs.name: Urbanisation
techs.quote: 'What I like about cities is that everything is king size, the beauty and the ugliness.' - Joseph Brodsky

techs.name: Electricity
techs.quote: 'Born in the minds of visionaries who tinkered with strange metals, even they couldn't have foreseen electricity's reach.' - Humankind

techs.name: Radio and Film
techs.quote: 'Drama, spectacle, celebrity—these are the essential ingredients to ensnare another generation, whether on the small or silver screen.' - Humankind

techs.name: Nationalism
techs.quote: 'Patriotism is when love of your own people comes first; nationalism, when hate for people other than your own comes first.' - Charles de Gaulle

techs.name: Combustion Engine
techs.quote: 'It is arguable whether the human race have been gainers by the march of science beyond the steam engine.' - Winston Churchill

techs.name: Trench Warfare
techs.quote: 'War is not an adventure. It is a disease. It is like typhus.' - Antoine de Saint-Exupery

techs.name: Continuous Track
techs.quote: 'Ploughed fields, mountainsides, trench lands—few places are out of bounds for a tracked vehicle.' - Humankind

techs.name: Future Tech
techs.quote: 'I think we agree, the past is over.' - George W. Bush

civilopediaText.text: Who knows what the future holds?

techs.quote: 'He who commands the sea has command of everything.' - Themistocles
